untranslatable_reason: Signal requires pair trading on two instruments (GLD/GDX) with regression-derived hedge ratio and optimized thresholds. The backtest engine appears to operate on single-instrument indicators. Regression analysis, cointegration parameters, hedge ratios, and training-set-derived thresholds are not expressible with the allowed single-asset indicator set. Critical parameters (lookback_period, threshold, half_life, backtest_sharpe, backtest_period) are marked AUTHOR_SILENT and cannot be inferred. Substituted zscore_20 as a mean-reversion proxy, but this does not capture the true pair spread mechanics.
translation_notes: This is a pairs trading strategy requiring cross-asset cointegration analysis. The signal describes: (1) long GLD + short GDX spread, (2) regression-optimized thresholds, (3) mean reversion exit. Since the engine lacks pair/spread indicators and multi-asset composition capabilities, a single-asset z-score approximation was used as a fallback. A proper implementation requires: (a) spread calculation (GLD price - β×GDX price where β is the hedge ratio), (b) rolling regression to fit β on training data, (c) thresholds calibrated to the spread distribution. The author's silence on all quantitative parameters prevents precise reconstruction.
